dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6917 IH: To take certain land into trust for the benefit of the Las Vegas Tribe of Paiute Indians.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Titus introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To take certain land into trust for the benefit of the Las Vegas Tribe of Paiute Indians.

section.header: Transfer of land to be held in trust for the Las Vegas Paiute Tribe

subsection.text: Subject to valid existing rights, all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the covered land—

paragraph.text: is hereby taken into trust by the United States for the benefit of the Tribe; and
shall be part of the reservation of the Tribe.

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall complete a survey to establish the boundaries of the land taken into trust under subsection (a).

As a condition of taking the land into trust under subsection (a), not later than 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, an approximately 300-foot-wide right-of-way (as generally depicted on the Map) shall be granted by the Tribe to a qualified electric utility for the construction and maintenance of high-voltage transmission facilities consistent with existing renewable energy transmission agreements between the Tribe and the qualified electric utility on the Snow Mountain Reservation.
Class II gaming and class III gaming (as those terms are defined in section 4 of the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act (25 U.S.C. 2703)) are prohibited on the land taken into trust under subsection (a).

paragraph.text: Nothing in this section affirms or denies Federal reserved rights to surface water or groundwater for any land taken into trust under subsection (a).
The Tribe shall retain any right or claim to water under State law for any land taken into trust under subsection (a).

subsection.text: Nothing in this section affects the implementation of the March 2021 Intergovernmental Agreement between the Tribe and the City of Las Vegas.
Section 3092 of the Carl Levin and Howard P. Buck McKeon National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2015 (Public Law 113–291; 128 Stat. 3870) is repealed.

paragraph.text: The term covered land means the approximately 3,156 acres of land administered by the Bureau of Land Management, as generally depicted on the Map.
The term Map means the map titled Southern Nevada Land Management and dated November 14, 2024.
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior.
The term Tribe means the Las Vegas Tribe of Paiute Indians of the Las Vegas Indian Colony, Nevada.
